result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  kiota [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  generate             Generates a REST HTTP API client from an OpenAPI description file.
  search <searchTerm>  Searches for an OpenAPI description in multiple registries.
  download <key>       Downloads an OpenAPI description from multiple registries.
  show                 Displays the API tree in a given description.
  info                 Displays information about the languages supported by kiota and dependencies to add in your project.
  update               Updates existing clients under the target directory using their lock files.
  login                Logs in to the Kiota registries so search/download/show/generate commands can access private API definitions.
  logout               Logs out of Kiota registries.
  rpc                  WARNING EXPERIMENTAL: Starts a kiota as a JSON-RPC server.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  kiota [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  generate             Generates a REST HTTP API client from an OpenAPI description file.
  search <searchTerm>  Searches for an OpenAPI description in multiple registries.
  download <key>       Downloads an OpenAPI description from multiple registries.
  show                 Displays the API tree in a given description.
  info                 Displays information about the languages supported by kiota and dependencies to add in your project.
  update               Updates existing clients under the target directory using their lock files.
  login                Logs in to the Kiota registries so search/download/show/generate commands can access private API definitions.
  logout               Logs out of Kiota registries.
  rpc                  WARNING EXPERIMENTAL: Starts a kiota as a JSON-RPC server.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Downloads an OpenAPI description from multiple registries.

Usage:
  kiota download <key> [options]

Arguments:
  <key>  The search result key to download the description for. Use the search command to get the key.

Options:
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Warning]
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                      Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  -v, --version <version>                                                  The version of the OpenAPI document to use. []
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Removes all files from the output directory before generating the code files. [default: False]
  -o, --output <path>                                                      The output directory path for the generated code files. [default: ./output/result.json]
  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                          Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                          Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates a REST HTTP API client from an OpenAPI description file.

Usage:
  kiota generate [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <path>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             The path or URI to the OpenAPI description file used to generate the code files.
  -a, --manifest <manifest>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        The path or URI to the API manifest file used to generate the code files. Append #apikey if the target manifest contains multiple API dependencies entries.
  -o, --output <path>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The output directory path for the generated code files. [default: ./output]
  -l, --language <CSharp|Dart|Go|HTTP|Java|PHP|Python|Ruby|TypeScript> (REQUIRED)                                                                                                                                                                                  The target language for the generated code files.
  -c, --class-name <name>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          The class name to use for the core client class. [default: ApiClient]
  --tam, --type-access-modifier <Internal|Private|Protected|Public>                                                                                                                                                                                                The type access modifier to use for the client types. [default: Public]
  -n, --namespace-name <name>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      The namespace to use for the core client class specified with the --class-name option. [default: ApiSdk]
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>                                                                                                                                                                                          The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Warning]
  -b, --backing-store                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Enables backing store for models. [default: False]
  --ebc, --exclude-backward-compatible                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Excludes backward compatible and obsolete assets from the generated result. Should be used for new clients. [default: False]
  --ad, --additional-data                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Will include the 'AdditionalData' property for models. [default: True]
  -s, --serializer <classes>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       The fully qualified class names for serializers. Accepts multiple values. Use `none` to generate a client without any serializer. [default: Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Json.JsonSerializationWriterFactory|Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Text.TextSerializationWriterFactory|Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Form.FormSerializationWriterFactory|Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Multipart.MultipartSerializationWriterFactory]
  --deserializer, --ds <classes>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   The fully qualified class names for deserializers. Accepts multiple values. Use `none` to generate a client without any deserializer. [default: Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Json.JsonParseNodeFactory|Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Text.TextParseNodeFactory|Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Form.FormParseNodeFactory]
  --clean-output, --co                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Removes all files from the output directory before generating the code files. [default: False]
  -m, --structured-mime-types <structured-mime-types>                                                                                                                                                                                                              The MIME types with optional priorities as defined in RFC9110 Accept header to use for structured data model generation. Accepts multiple values. [default: application/json|text/plain;q=0.9|application/x-www-form-urlencoded;q=0.2|multipart/form-data;q=0.1]
  -i, --include-path <include-path>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                The paths to include in the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to include. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  -e, --exclude-path <exclude-path>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                The paths to exclude from the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to exclude. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  --disable-validation-rules, --dvr <DivergentResponseSchema,GetWithBody,InconsistentTypeFormatPair,KnownAndNotSupportedFormats,MissingDiscriminator,MultipleServerEntries,NoContentWithBody,NoServerEntry,UrlFormEncodedComplex,ValidationRuleSetExtensions,All>  The OpenAPI description validation rules to disable. Accepts multiple values. []
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Show help and usage information

Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to include. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []

Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to exclude. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  --disable-validation-rules, --dvr <DivergentResponseSchema,GetWithBody,InconsistentTypeFormatPair,KnownAndNotSupportedFormats,MissingDiscriminator,MultipleServerEntries,NoContentWithBody,NoServerEntry,UrlFormEncodedComplex,ValidationRuleSetExtensions,All>  The OpenAPI description validation rules to disable. Accepts multiple values. []
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Displays information about the languages supported by kiota and dependencies to add in your project.

Usage:
  kiota info [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <path>                                                                        The path or URI to the OpenAPI description file used to generate the code files.
  -a, --manifest <manifest>                                                                   The path or URI to the API manifest file used to generate the code files. Append #apikey if the target manifest contains multiple API dependencies entries.
  -v, --version <version>                                                                     The version of the OpenAPI document to use. []
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>                     The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Warning]
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                                         Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  -k, --search-key <search-key>                                                               The API search key to display the description for. Use the search command to get the key. []
  -l, --language <CSharp|Dart|Go|HTTP|Java|PHP|Python|Ruby|TypeScript>                        The target language for the dependencies instructions.
  --json                                                                                      Generate a plain and machine-parsable json output.
  --dependency-type, --dt <Abstractions|Additional|Authentication|Bundle|Http|Serialization>  The type of dependency to display instructions for. []
  -?, -h, --help                                                                              Show help and usage information

  --json                                                                                      Generate a plain and machine-parsable json output.

  -?, -h, --help                                                                              Show help and usage information

Description:
  Logs in to the Kiota registries so search/download/show/generate commands can access private API definitions.

Usage:
  kiota login [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  github  Logs in to GitHub.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Logs in to GitHub.

Usage:
  kiota login github [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:

  pat     Logs in to GitHub using a Personal Access Token.

Usage:
  kiota rpc [options]

Options:
  -m, --mode <NamedPipe|Stdio>  Whether the RPC server should use stdin/stdout or a named pipe. [default: Stdio]
  -p, --pipe-name <pipe-name>   The name of the named pipe to use for the RPC server. [default: KiotaJsonRpc]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Searches for an OpenAPI description in multiple registries.

Usage:
  kiota search <searchTerm> [options]

Arguments:
  <searchTerm>  The term to search for.

Options:
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Warning]
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                      Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  -v, --version <version>                                                  The version of the OpenAPI document to use. []
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Displays the API tree in a given description.

Usage:
  kiota show [options]

Options:
  -k, --search-key <search-key>                                            The API search key to display the description for. Use the search command to get the key. []
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Warning]
  -v, --version <version>                                                  The version of the OpenAPI document to use. []
  -d, --openapi <path>                                                     The path or URI to the OpenAPI description file used to generate the code files.
  -a, --manifest <manifest>                                                The path or URI to the API manifest file used to generate the code files. Append #apikey if the target manifest contains multiple API dependencies entries.
  --m-d, --max-depth <max-depth>                                           The maximum depth of the tree to display [default: 5]
  -i, --include-path <include-path>                                        The paths to include in the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to include. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  -e, --exclude-path <exclude-path>                                        The paths to exclude from the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to exclude. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                      Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                          Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --m-d, --max-depth <max-depth>                                           The maximum depth of the tree to display [default: 5]
  -i, --include-path <include-path>                                        The paths to include in the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to include. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  -e, --exclude-path <exclude-path>                                        The paths to exclude from the generation. Glob patterns accepted. Accepts multiple values. Append #OPERATION to the pattern to specify the operation to exclude. e.g. users/*/messages#GET []
  --cc, --clear-cache                                                      Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
  --disable-ssl-validation, --dsv                                          Disables SSL certificate validation. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

  --cc, --clear-cache                                                      Clears any cached data for the current command. [default: False]
